rationale: Recent shutdown / wind-down batch covering Balancer, Polynomial Protocol, and Step Finance / Remora Markets. Balancer is modeled as limited because Balancer Labs is winding down after the 2025 exploit while the Balancer protocol is expected to continue under a DAO/Foundation/OpCo structure. Polynomial Protocol is modeled as dead / voluntary_shutdown because Polynomial Chain and Polynomial Trade entered a dated final wind-down with operations ceased, forced liquidation, liquidity-layer closure, and chain shutdown. Step Finance is modeled as dead / hack because its treasury breach led to immediate wind-down of Step Finance, SolanaFloor, and Remora Markets.

entities.summary: Decentralized exchange and automated-market-maker protocol. In March 2026, Balancer Labs, the corporate entity historically associated with the protocol, announced it would wind down after the November 2025 Balancer v2 exploit, while the protocol itself was expected to continue through DAO/Foundation/OpCo structures.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The public record supports Balancer Labs winding down and a major exploit history, but the protocol continuation path is explicitly described in the official forum post and secondary coverage. Treat this as limited / restructuring unless later evidence shows protocol-level shutdown.

entities.summary: DeFi derivatives protocol and trading stack that announced the shutdown of Polynomial Chain and Polynomial Trade in February 2026, with operations ceased, forced liquidations, liquidity-layer closure, and chain shutdown scheduled by March 3, 2026.
entities.notes: Modeled as dead / voluntary_shutdown. Sources report a dated wind-down rather than an exploit, insolvency proceeding, or enforcement shutdown. Confidence remains medium because current evidence set is mainly syndicated / mirrored reporting of the team announcement rather than a directly archived official page.

entities.summary: Solana DeFi dashboard / aggregator group whose linked operations included SolanaFloor and the Remora Markets trading/RWA platform. It announced a wind-down of operations after a January 2026 treasury breach that drained tens of millions of dollars and left the team unable to secure financing or an acquisition.
entities.notes: Modeled as hybrid because Step Finance itself is a DeFi dashboard/aggregator while Remora Markets adds trading/RWA exposure. The terminal marker is the group wind-down announcement following the treasury breach. Loss figures vary by report, so amounts are described in event/evidence notes rather than hard-coded as a numeric field.

events.title: Balancer v2 pools suffered a major exploit
events.description: Balancer v2 pools were exploited across multiple chains in November 2025, with secondary reports describing approximately USD 128 million drained and the flaw later cited as a source of legal exposure for Balancer Labs.
events.notes: Use exploit rather than hack because reporting attributes the loss to a protocol-level flaw in Balancer v2 logic.

events.title: Balancer Labs announced it would wind down
events.description: Balancer co-founder Fernando Martinelli announced that Balancer Labs would wind down, while explicitly stating that he was not calling for a full Balancer protocol shutdown and supported a lean continuation path.
events.notes: Corporate entity wind-down only. Do not treat as protocol death.

events.title: Polynomial Protocol announced final wind-down
events.description: Polynomial announced the closure of Polynomial Chain and Polynomial Trade, cancellation of its planned Q1 2026 TGE, and a phased wind-down including forced liquidation, liquidity-layer closure, and chain shutdown.
events.notes: Announcement date follows syndicated PANews / exchange-news reporting.

events.title: Polynomial forced-liquidation phase began
events.description: Polynomial's wind-down timeline scheduled forced liquidations for February 18, 2026 after operations ceased on February 14.
events.notes: Used as the trading/position termination marker within the wind-down sequence.

events.title: Polynomial Chain shutdown date reached
events.description: The final timeline for Polynomial's wind-down scheduled chain operations to halt on March 3, 2026, after liquidity-layer closure on February 24.
events.notes: HEI uses the chain-shutdown date as the terminal marker for the protocol/trading stack.

events.title: Step Finance suffered a major treasury breach
events.description: Step Finance suffered a treasury-wallet breach in late January 2026. Reports place the loss in a broad USD 26-40 million range depending on source and accounting for recovered or affected Remora assets.
events.notes: Loss amounts vary; avoid a single definitive figure in entity-level fields.

events.title: Step Finance announced group wind-down
events.description: Step Finance announced that Step Finance, SolanaFloor, and Remora Markets would wind down after failing to secure financing, acquisition, or another viable recovery path following the breach.
events.notes: Announcement date follows the project statement as reported by The Block, Cointelegraph, and other sources.

events.title: Step Finance treated as closed
events.description: Because the wind-down was described as immediate cessation of operations, HEI uses the announcement date as the effective closure marker for the Step Finance / Remora Markets group record.
events.notes: User recovery / buyback processes may continue after the platform operation marker.

evidence.title: On the Future of Balancer: Shutting Down Balancer Labs, Supporting the Path Forward
evidence.notes: Primary source for Balancer Labs wind-down, protocol-continuation language, and the relationship between the November 2025 exploit and the corporate wind-down.

evidence.title: Balancer Labs to shut down after $128 million exploit; protocol eyes 'lean' restructuring
evidence.notes: Secondary source confirming corporate wind-down, protocol continuation, and approximate exploit amount.

evidence.title: Balancer Labs to Shut Down Post $128M Exploit: Here's What It Plans to do Next
evidence.notes: Supporting source for exploit amount, corporate shutdown, and DAO/OpCo transition context.

evidence.title: DeFi derivatives protocol Polynomial to cease operations and shut down its chain and trading products
evidence.notes: Used for the Polynomial Chain / Trade shutdown timeline, operations-ceased marker, forced-liquidation date, liquidity-layer closure, and chain shutdown date.

evidence.title: DeFi derivatives protocol Polynomial will cease operations and shut down its chain and trading products
evidence.notes: Secondary mirror confirming the same wind-down dates and TGE cancellation.

evidence.title: Polynomial Shuts Down DeFi Derivatives Platform, Cancels Token Launch And Initiates Withdrawal Deadline For Users
evidence.notes: Supporting source for public shutdown framing and user deadline context.

evidence.title: Step Finance shuts down following $40 million security breach
evidence.notes: Used for the Step Finance wind-down, security-breach amount, and impact on SolanaFloor and Remora Markets.

evidence.title: 3 Solana platforms to shutter following devastating $40M hack
evidence.notes: Used for Step Finance / SolanaFloor / Remora Markets group closure and the $40M updated figure.

evidence.title: Step Finance Unable to Recover From $26M Attack, Announces Shutdown
evidence.notes: Supporting source for the lower $26M estimate, unrecoverable-attack framing, and recovery/buyback context.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm no existing Balancer, Polynomial Protocol, or Step Finance record before canonical apply.
Do not mark Balancer dead; Balancer Labs is winding down while the protocol continuation path is explicitly supported.
Apply Polynomial as dead / voluntary_shutdown with March 3, 2026 as terminal chain-shutdown marker.
Apply Step Finance as dead / hack, but preserve loss amount uncertainty in notes.
Use event ids hei_ev_000361 through hei_ev_000368 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000766 through hei_src_000774 only if available.
